threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look startled when the Tube's delayed—locals just grab a coffee and roll with it.
Caught a random busker on the 45 last night, he played ‘Wonderwall’ on a ukulele, and somehow it turned into a singalong. Never seen so many smiles at 3 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I stumbled upon a street art piece of a cat wearing a beret, holding a coffee cup. Classic Neukölln, blending the absurd with the everyday.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous block parties where everyone brings a dish to share, and the kids kick a ball in the street without a care.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they roll their eyes at tourists waiting for BART to announce a delay. It's like, welcome to the club, buddy.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus speeding past a guy juggling flaming torches at Eddy & Jones. He didn’t miss a beat when the driver honked. Classic Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but it’s the hidden squares and local bars where the real soul of the neighborhood hangs out.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they still struggle with the 'll' sound in 'Barcelona'. That awkward 'y' slip gives it away every time.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can rely on the Tube at rush hour. Locals know it’s the buses that do the heavy lifting, if they’re running at all.
Heard a bloke on the 77 last night arguing with a stranger about the best fried chicken in Brixton. It was like a debate club at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a toddler playing with a stray cat outside the Spätkauf, completely unfazed by the noise of the tram. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a lawn chair library on the canal, where locals drop off unused chairs for others to lounge. Such a chill vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows that 16th Street BART is a game of roulette. Plan your trip with at least a 30-minute buffer for delays, or you're asking for trouble.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The other day at Civic Center, a dog casually strolled across the tracks, while Muni drivers had to signal a 10-minute delay. Gotta love this city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the plazas, but they miss the tiny, tucked-away bars where the real locals gather after dark.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their unwavering confidence in using the metro during an afternoon siesta—everyone else knows it's a ghost town.
